steps.title: Rewrite the radicals
steps.content:
Use fractional exponents for the cube root and fourth root.
$$\sqrt[3]{126n}=(126n)^{1/3}$$
$$\sqrt[4]{126n}=(126n)^{1/4}$$

steps.title: Combine the exponents
steps.content:
The fourth-root factor is squared, so multiply that exponent by $2$. Then add exponents because the bases match.
$$(126n)^{1/3}((126n)^{1/4})^2=(126n)^{1/3}(126n)^{1/2}$$
$$=(126n)^{5/6}$$

steps.title: Solve for the value
steps.content:
Set the exponent equal to $18x$ and divide by $18$.
$$18x=\frac{5}{6}$$
$$x=\frac{5}{6}\cdot\frac{1}{18}=\frac{5}{108}$$
The answer is 5/108.
